weekly_summary: Low-volume base-building week focused on aerobic foundation and consistency. With only 10 km logged in the past 14 days, this week prioritizes easy running to safely rebuild volume toward the 4-hour weekly budget.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest day. Focus on sleep, hydration, and nutrient-rich meals to support the week ahead.
days.notes: Given low recent volume, prioritize quality sleep and nutrition before training week begins.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: Conversational pace, easy effort. Should be able to speak full sentences throughout. Nasal breathing if possible.
days.notes: Run by feel, not pace. Target effort: 60% max HR equivalent. Gradual volume increase from recent low baseline.

days.title: Active Recovery or Rest
days.description: Complete rest or optional very easy cross-training (walking, swimming, yoga). No running today.
days.notes: Not a preferred running day. Allows body to adapt to Monday's stimulus.

days.title: Half Marathon Pace Tempo
days.description: 5 min easy warm-up + 8 × 90 seconds at half-marathon goal pace (6:00/km) with 90 sec jog recovery between reps. 3 min cool-down easy.
days.key_focus: Half-marathon specific pace, lactate threshold development
days.notes: Target HM pace ~6:00/km for 2:00 HM. Focus on rhythm and controlled effort. This is your intensity anchor for the week.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest day. Gentle activity optional (walking, stretching) but no running.
days.notes: Day after hard session. Prioritize sleep and protein intake.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest day. Focus on hydration and light meal prep for Saturday long run.
days.notes: Not a preferred running day. Allows full recovery before long run.

days.title: Long Run — Aerobic Build
days.description: Steady-state easy pace, conversational effort. This is a comfortable distance to build aerobic capacity. Should finish feeling like you could continue.
days.notes: Run by effort/HR, not pace. Target 65-75% max HR. This is your long run anchor; expect to reach ~13 km for a 2:00 HM goal. 13 km at easy pace is roughly 85 min of total running time.

coaching_notes: You've logged only 10 km over the past 14 days, indicating a low-baseline period. This week deliberately ramps volume conservatively (32 km total, 240 min) to safely rebuild fitness without injury risk. The week follows 80/20 polarization: 22 km easy (88%), 10 km at tempo/HM pace (12%). Wednesday's tempo work establishes your half-marathon pace anchor (6:00/km), and Saturday's 13 km long run begins building aerobic endurance. Total training time: ~240 min (4 hours), matching your weekly budget. Expect to feel stronger by week's end as consistency returns.
recovery_flags: Low training volume in last 14 days — monitor for rapid adaptation and soreness; scale back if excessive fatigue appears.
No HR data available from recent runs — use Rate of Perceived Exertion (RPE) and ability to hold a conversation as intensity guides.
Gradual volume increase is intentional; do not jump to higher mileage abruptly.
next_week_preview: Week 2 will introduce a second moderate intensity session (Tempo or Progressive Long Run) and nudge long run distance toward 14–15 km. Total volume will increase to ~40 km as aerobic base solidifies. Focus on consistent easy runs and one quality hard effort per week.
